records.title: ops.ingest_run normalized run contract
records.statement: All ingest writers must use one run-id lifecycle contract with canonical statuses.
acceptance_criteria: Every writer uses run_id for insert/update lifecycle rows.
Status vocabulary is canonical across SQL and TS writers.
Error paths produce terminal telemetry rows.

records.title: No /api/cron requirement in auth/gates
records.statement: Middleware and gates must align to pg_cron-only architecture without /api/cron assumptions.
acceptance_criteria: No route-level auth exception depends on /api/cron path.
Verification gate validates architecture policy without route-presence checks.

records.title: Intraday fallback contract fidelity
records.statement: Intraday route comments and implementation must agree on fallback semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Fallback is implemented when claimed, or claim is removed.
Response metadata makes active source path explicit.

records.title: Envelope semantics parity across contract/routes/gate
records.functional_section: API Envelope and Parity Contract Consistency
records.statement: API contract types, route payloads, and parity checks must agree on required and optional envelope fields.
acceptance_criteria: Optional warning field remains optional everywhere.
Shared envelope shape is enforced consistently by route and gate surfaces.

records.title: Mixed-vintage freshness transparency
records.statement: Risk-factor freshness must expose mixed-vintage state without misleading canonical freshness labels.
acceptance_criteria: Payload includes as_of_date_min, as_of_date_max, and mixed_vintage.
UI freshness messaging reflects degraded mixed-vintage mode explicitly.

records.title: Forecast horizon normalization parity
records.statement: All forecast-facing endpoints must apply identical horizon normalization logic.
acceptance_criteria: Endpoints share AG_HORIZON_DAYS allowlist and LEGACY_HORIZON_MAP values.
Legacy remap and unknown-horizon filtering behavior are equivalent.
